messages.content:
I have a prestylized text layer on After Effects. Say its name is "01".
I need a script which imports txt file and creates multiple comps
for each line separated with "*****". (Comp names should be as "Text01", "Text02",...
Such as;
This is text 01
*****
This is text 02
*****
So script should create 2 comps and replace text content with these strings.
Original comp should be duplicated and text layer (and its style) should remain same. We basically just need to update text on other comps.
Also let's make it with an user interface so we can dock it in our layout. We can include "select txt file" button and "run" button to UI.

It says : "No text blocks found or source composition not found."
My txt file content is like:
This is a test 01
*****
This is a test 02
*****
This is a test 03
*****
This is a test 04

I have a comp in after effects and there is a single text layer nothing else.
It is throwing an error on this line:             var textBlock = textBlocks[i].trim();
It nows gives an error on this line:         textArray = text.split("*****").map(function(block) {

It still gives an error for this line:         textArray = text.replace(/
/g, "
").split("
").map(function (block) {

It still gives an error on this line: (I am sure that my text layer name is 01)         textArray = text.replace(/
/g, "
").split("
").map(function (block) {
If this delimeter creates a conflict we can replace it with another thing. Such as --- or something.
